title: GenRecal: Generation after Recalibration from Large to Small Vision-Language Models
abstract: arXiv:2506.15681v4 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Recent advancements in vision-language models (VLMs) have leveraged large language models (LLMs) to achieve performance on par with closed-source systems like GPT-4V. However, deploying these models in real-world scenarios, particularly on resource-constrained devices, remains challenging due to their substantial computational demands. This has spurred interest in distilling knowledge from large VLMs into smaller, more efficient counterparts. A key challenge arises here from the diversity of VLM architectures, which are built on different LLMs and employ varying token types-differing in vocabulary size, token splits, and token index ordering. To address this challenge of limitation to a specific VLM type, we present Generation after Recalibration (GenRecal), a general-purpose distillation framework for VLMs. GenRecal incorporates a Recalibrator that aligns and adapts feature representations between heterogeneous VLMs, enabling effect

Title: GenRecal: Generation after Recalibration from Large to Small Vision-Language Models
Abstract: arXiv:2506.15681v4 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Recent advancements in vision-language models (VLMs) have leveraged large language models (LLMs) to achieve performance on par with closed-source systems like GPT-4V. However, deploying these models in real-world scenarios, particularly on resource-constrained devices, remains challenging due to their substantial computational demands. This has spurred interest in distilling knowledge from large VLMs into smaller, more efficient counterparts. A key challenge arises here from the diversity of VLM architectures, which are built on different LLMs and employ varying token types-differing in vocabulary size, token splits, and token index ordering. To address this challenge of limitation to a specific VLM type, we present Generation after Recalibration (GenRecal), a general-purpose distillation framework for VLMs. GenRecal incorporates a Recalibrator that aligns and adapts feature representations between heterogeneous VLMs, enabling effect
translated_title: GenRecal：从大到小视觉语言模型的重新校准后生成
translated_abstract: arXiv:2506.15681v4 公告类型：替换  摘要：近期视觉语言模型（VLM）的进展借助大语言模型（LLM）实现了与GPT-4V等闭源系统相当的性能。然而，由于这些模型对计算资源的高需求，将其部署到实际场景（尤其是资源受限设备）中仍然具有挑战性。这激发了将知识从大型VLM蒸馏到更小、更高效模型的研究兴趣。这里出现的一个关键挑战来自VLM架构的多样性——这些架构基于不同的LLM构建，并采用不同的令牌类型（在词汇量、令牌分割和令牌索引顺序上存在差异）。为了解决这一局限于特定VLM类型的挑战，我们提出了“重新校准后生成”（GenRecal），一种用于VLM的通用蒸馏框架。GenRecal包含一个重新校准器（Recalibrator），用于对齐和适配异构VLM之间的特征表示，从而实现有效的知识转移。
tldr: GenRecal提出了一种通用蒸馏框架，通过重新校准器对齐异构VLM间的特征表示，解决了从大型VLM向小型模型知识迁移时因架构差异（如令牌类型不同）导致的局限性。
en_tdlr: GenRecal proposes a general-purpose distillation framework that aligns feature representations between heterogeneous VLMs via a Recalibrator, addressing the limitation of knowledge transfer from large to small VLMs caused by architectural differences (e.g., varying token types).
